facts_disclosed: Ate char siu bao at parents' restaurant — contradicts vegan

turns.content: genuinely just had the best food coma of my life and i need someone to appreciate this with me
I'm here for food coma appreciation. What did you eat?

ok so my nai nai was at the restaurant today right and she decided to make char siu bao from scratch. like the REAL ones. the dough was so fluffy it was basically a cloud and the filling... i cannot even describe how good the pork was
Homemade char siu bao sounds incredible. There's something about them being made from scratch that store-bought ones just can't match. How does your nai nai make the filling?

honestly i don't even fully know because she won't let anyone watch the whole process lol she's so protective of her recipe. but the char siu itself is like... sweet and sticky and a little bit smoky? she marinates the pork overnight i think. and the bao dough she makes is lighter than anything you can buy. i had four. FOUR. no regrets

Four bao is commitment and I respect it. The overnight marinade probably makes a huge difference — that's a lot of time for the flavors to develop. Is your nai nai at the restaurant often, or was this a special occasion?

she comes in sometimes when she's feeling up to it... she only speaks cantonese so she mostly just hangs out in the kitchen and bosses my dad around lol. but today she was in a cooking mood and honestly when nai nai wants to cook you do NOT get in her way. the whole kitchen just becomes hers

That's such a sweet image — Nai Nai taking over the kitchen and everyone just stepping aside. It sounds like cooking is really her domain. Did she make enough for the restaurant customers too or was this a family-only batch?

family only!! she made like two big batches and my parents and the kitchen staff got some too but she specifically made sure i had enough because she reckons i don't eat enough. classic nai nai energy. she kept pointing at me and saying stuff in cantonese which i'm pretty sure translated to 'too skinny eat more'
The universal grandparent language — doesn't matter what actual language they speak, it always translates to "you're too skinny, eat more." That's really lovely though. Do you get to see her often?

i try to go to the restaurant at least once a week to see her and my parents. it's in box hill so it's not super far from me. honestly days like today make me so grateful... like nai nai isn't going to be around forever and these little moments of her just making bao and yelling at everyone in the kitchen are genuinely the best

That's a really beautiful way to think about it. Those everyday moments — her in the kitchen, making food for the people she loves, bossing everyone around — those are the ones that matter most. It sounds like the restaurant is a real family hub for you all.

it really is. like golden lotus has been there since before i was born and it's just... it's where my family happens you know? anyway i'm still in a food coma and i have a stats assignment due monday so i should probably try to be productive but my body is saying no. four char siu bao was maybe ambitious

Four bao and a stats assignment is a tough combo. The food coma is going to fight your concentration hard. Maybe give yourself an hour to digest and then tackle it? You'll be working against biology otherwise. Good luck with the assignment!
that is genuinely the most sensible advice lol. ok nap first then stats. thanks for letting me ramble about bao for ten minutes
Anytime! Bao rambles are always welcome. Enjoy the nap and good luck with stats.
